meaning: v.包围,围绕,使烦恼 vbl.包围,围绕
examples: A United Nations aid convoy loaded with food and medicine finally got through to the besieged town.
She was besieged by the press and the public.
The actress was besieged by reporters at the airport.
Hess, besieged by billionaire hedge fund manager Paul Singer, is attempting a similar maneuver.
Others are clueless and besieged by new-media providers--some legitimate, some fly-by-night--clamoring for their attention.
